examples.title: Basic Pointer Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates pointer declaration, initialization, and dereferencing
examples.explanation: Shows how to declare a pointer, initialize it with address of variable using & operator, and access/modify value using * (dereference operator)

examples.title: Pointer Arithmetic with Arrays
examples.description: Shows how pointer arithmetic traverses array elements
examples.explanation: Demonstrates that arr[i] is equivalent to *(arr+i). Shows pointer arithmetic where adding 1 to int* moves by sizeof(int) bytes. Also shows difference between *p++ (post-increment) and (*p)++ (increment value).

examples.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation
examples.description: Allocates memory at runtime using malloc() and demonstrates proper memory management
examples.explanation: Shows dynamic memory allocation using malloc(). Points to note: (int*) is type cast, sizeof(int) ensures portability, checking NULL prevents dereferencing invalid memory, free() releases memory to prevent leaks.

examples.title: Pointer to Pointer (Double Pointer)
examples.description: Demonstrates how double pointers can modify a pointer value inside a function
examples.explanation: Shows double pointer usage: allocateMemory receives address of pointer (&p), so it can modify the original pointer. Also demonstrates swapping two pointers. Double pointer is essential when you need to modify the pointer itself from within a function.
